description: Execute short, sandboxed code snippets in Python, Bash, or Node.js. Network access is denied; use only for computation, file manipulation in /tmp, or tooling that does not require external calls.

tools.name: run_python
tools.description: Execute a Python code snippet and return stdout/stderr.
tools.returns: Combined stdout/stderr output, with likely secrets redacted.
tools.whenToUse: Use for data transformation, parsing, math, or any logic best expressed in Python without network access.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for running shell commands or for code that needs network access, secrets, or persistent state outside /tmp.

tools.name: run_bash
tools.description: Execute a Bash command and return stdout/stderr.
tools.returns: Combined stdout/stderr output, with likely secrets redacted.
tools.whenToUse: Use for shell-based file inspection, text processing with grep/awk, or invoking installed CLI tools locally.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for network operations, privilege escalation, writing outside /tmp, or interactive commands.

tools.name: run_node
tools.description: Execute a Node.js code snippet and return stdout/stderr.
tools.returns: Combined stdout/stderr output, with likely secrets redacted.
tools.whenToUse: Use when the task is easiest in JavaScript/Node.js, such as JSON manipulation or regex operations.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for network access, requiring npm packages, or persistent state outside /tmp.
